The data presented in the table offers critical insights into the developmental stages of gustatory neurons in the mouse model,
particularly focusing on key metrics such as "Peak cell production" and "Axons reach tongue." Understanding the timeline of
these developmental milestones is essential for elucidating the processes underlying taste system formation. For instance, the
variable "Peak cell production" indicates the optimal period during which neuronal populations expand, while "Axons reach

tongue" signifies the crucial juncture at which these neurons establish connections necessary for taste sensation. 

The findings
are particularly relevant to the stages of development at embryonic days E8.5, E9.5, and E10.5, which highlight the dynamic
progression of placode formation and subsequent delamination migration. By analyzing the frequency and percent distributions
of these variables, researchers can better appreciate the intricacies of neuronal development in relation to both time and cellular
behavior. This information not only enhances our understanding of gustatory system maturation but also informs potential
avenues for further investigation into neural development and related disorders.
